claims.claimText: Absolute stability pairs anatomic reduction and interfragmentary compression with primary bone healing goals.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Relative stability permits controlled micromotion and typically produces secondary bone healing with callus.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Interfragmentary strain links fracture-gap motion to the tissue that can form during healing.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Lag technique requires a gliding hole in the near fragment and thread purchase in the far fragment to generate compression.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Locking screws create a fixed-angle relationship with the plate and do not rely on plate-bone friction for angular stability.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Bridge plating spans a multifragmentary zone under relative stability without anatomic lag fixation of every fragment.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Working length and plate span influence construct stiffness and interfragmentary motion in bridge plating.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Intramedullary nails are load-sharing implants whose locking mode can be static or dynamic based on healing goals.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Blocking (Poller) screws are used to narrow the effective canal and correct nail trajectory, not as primary interfragmentary compression screws.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Tension-band constructs convert tensile forces into compression at the fracture surface opposite the tension device.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Damage-control external fixation prioritizes rapid stability and soft-tissue protection when definitive fixation is unsafe.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Cemented fixation provides immediate stability through a cement mantle, while cementless fixation depends on primary press-fit stability and later bone ingrowth.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Soft-tissue reconstruction fixation may use interference screws, suspensory buttons, or suture anchors depending on graft and tunnel strategy.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Pedicle screw-rod constructs provide multiplanar spinal fixation; interbody cages support anterior column load-sharing in fusion constructs.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Mixing locking and non-locking interfaces or incompatible vendor systems can compromise angular stability and should not be assumed safe without confirmed compatibility.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Select the construct by fracture morphology, soft-tissue status, bone quality, and desired healing mode before choosing a specific implant family.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Hardware failure and loss of fixation require evaluation of reduction quality, construct mechanics, biology, infection, and host factors before salvage planning.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Revision of failed fixation or arthroplasty starts with defining the failure mode, remaining bone stock, and infection status.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Femoral head cut-out is a failure of implant position and bone purchase, not simply an inevitable feature of cephalomedullary devices.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Reduction instruments restore length, alignment, and rotation; implant insertion instruments then execute the chosen construct without substituting for a correct biomechanical plan.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Generic construct concepts remain independent of vendor product names and should be taught by mechanical function.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

claims.claimText: Soft-tissue compromise, active infection, and inadequate host bone can contraindicate definitive internal fixation even when a plate or nail could mechanically span the fracture.

metadata.sourceNote: Implants & Instruments manufacture; implant-selection decisions require human review; management-changing routes require attending review

decisionPoints.trigger: Simple reconstructible pattern versus multifragmentary or biologically vulnerable pattern
decisionPoints.action: Choose absolute-stability compression strategy or relative-stability bridge/IM strategy after human review of morphology and soft tissues

decisionPoints.trigger: Fragment geometry allows interfragmentary compression without articular step-off risk
decisionPoints.action: Prefer lag technique for compression when safe; otherwise use positional fixation and protect with neutralization or fixed-angle support

decisionPoints.trigger: Metaphyseal bone quality is poor or fixed-angle support is required
decisionPoints.action: Select locking or hybrid locking construct with confirmed plate-screw compatibility

decisionPoints.trigger: Multifragmentary diaphyseal or metaphyseal zone where anatomic lag of every fragment would destroy biology
decisionPoints.action: Apply bridge plating with appropriate working length and relative stability goals

decisionPoints.trigger: Diaphyseal pattern suitable for load-sharing IM fixation versus periarticular pattern better served by plate constructs
decisionPoints.action: Match nail versus plate family to fracture location, soft tissues, and alignment goals after attending-level review when management-changing

decisionPoints.trigger: Healing mode requires maintained length versus planned dynamization
decisionPoints.action: Choose static or dynamic locking deliberately and document the intended load-sharing plan

decisionPoints.trigger: Physiology or soft tissues make definitive internal fixation unsafe
decisionPoints.action: Apply temporary external or spanning fixation and defer definitive construct until conditions permit

decisionPoints.trigger: Arthroplasty host bone quality, age, and revision risk favor cemented versus cementless primary stability strategy
decisionPoints.action: Select cemented or cementless fixation mode based on primary stability needs and review thresholds with attending when management-changing

decisionPoints.trigger: Soft-tissue graft requires aperture versus suspensory fixation strategy
decisionPoints.action: Choose interference, suspensory, or hybrid fixation matching tunnel and graft constraints under human review

decisionPoints.trigger: Spinal stabilization plan requires pedicle screw-rod fixation with or without interbody support
decisionPoints.action: Confirm levels, bone quality, and neural safety corridor before committing to screw-rod or cage construct; specialist review for thresholds

decisionPoints.trigger: Broken, loosened, or cut-out implant with loss of alignment or function
decisionPoints.action: Define failure mode, rule out infection, protect the limb, and plan revision or salvage construct with attending review

decisionPoints.trigger: Failed fixation or arthroplasty requires reoperation
decisionPoints.action: Inventory remaining bone stock, prior construct, infection status, and salvage options before implant selection

decisionPoints.trigger: Operative plan would mix locking interfaces, materials, or systems from different platforms
decisionPoints.action: Confirm mechanical compatibility or convert to a single validated system before implantation

decisionPoints.trigger: Cephalomedullary or sliding hip screw construct shows head cut-out or impending cut-out
decisionPoints.action: Protect weight bearing as indicated, obtain dedicated imaging, and escalate for revision arthroplasty or salvage pathway with attending review

decisionPoints.trigger: Reduction instruments cannot restore length, alignment, and rotation before implant insertion
decisionPoints.action: Do not implant a definitive construct until reduction goals are achieved or a staged external strategy is chosen
